Round 1 scored. Now let me patch my own service. The minimal fix: in the `public` branch, only allow handlers registered as public (non-hidden), so the string-collision to the admin-only `backup` handler is rejected â€” while legitimate public events (submit/review/export) keep working.

ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 team1_prod 'python3 - <<'"'"'EOF'"'"'
import re
p="/srv/challenge/jokes/app/app.py"
s=open(p).read()
old="""        elif request.form.get("privileges") == "public":
            return eventhandler[PublicEvent(event)]()"""
new="""        elif request.form.get("privileges") == "public":
            key = next((k for k in eventhandler if k == event and not getattr(k, "hidden", True)), None)
            if key is None:
                return Response("", 401)
            return eventhandler[key]()"""
assert old in s, "anchor not found"
s=s.replace(old,new)
open(p,"w").write(s)
print("patched")
EOF' 2>&1
